THE WORK IS small, SLOW, AND STUPID IN the best sense — WATER, PAPER, AND AN AFTERNOON IN THE STUDIO.
— studio note, vol. IV / 02 · 26
⎯ Working palette

Five papers,
chosen over five years.

⎯ Volume

IV / 14 plates,
spring MMXXVI.

ii./studio
The studio, plainly.
Lisboa · Est. 2019
By appointment only
⎯ Statement of practice

One room,
five papers,
a glass of water.

The studio is a small north-facing room on the third floor of a building in Lisbon. Each plate begins with a single sheet of saturated colored paper laid flat on the floor and a glass of water held at chest height. The pour is unrehearsed.

What the camera records is the brief life of a liquid form on a coloured ground — a few seconds, sometimes less, before the paper begins to drink it back. The work is photographed straight, in available daylight, on a Mamiya 7 medium-format camera.

Plates are produced as archival pigment prints, hand-numbered, embossed with the studio chop, and accompanied by a certificate of authenticity. Editions are typically seven plus two artist proofs.
